applicabilityConditions: A continuing cash-return commitment is supportable within an explicit authorization ceiling and capital flexibility remains adequate.
recommendedAction: Authorize bounded repurchase capacity while retaining discretion over actual deployment.
nonApplicabilityConditions: Competing investment or liquidity needs dominate the cash-return commitment.
guardrails: Treat the authorization as a ceiling rather than a requirement to deploy the full amount.
exceptions: Defer or reduce repurchases when capital flexibility or durable investment needs are more valuable.
reversalSignals: Liquidity tightens, higher-return investment needs emerge, or repurchase economics deteriorate.
